entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop known for its relative tolerance to soil salinity compared to other crops like maize and legumes.

entities.name: SOIL SALINITY
entities.description: A soil condition characterized by high salt concentrations that can inhibit plant growth.

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A crop that is less tolerant to soil salinity than sorghum.

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: A group of crops that are less tolerant to soil salinity than sorghum.

entities.name: SALINITY TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant genotype to withstand and grow in saline soil conditions.

entities.name: GENETIC VARIATION
entities.description: The interspecific and intra-specific differences in salinity tolerance among crop genotypes.

entities.name: MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural techniques used in conjunction with tolerant genotypes to exploit saline soils.

relationships.description: Sorghum exhibits relative tolerance to salinity.
Sorghum is more tolerant to soil salinity than maize or legumes.
Maize is less tolerant to soil salinity than sorghum.
Legumes are less tolerant to soil salinity than sorghum.
Salinity tolerance exhibits significant interspecific and intra-specific variation.
Genetic variation in tolerance provides a scope for integration with management practices.
Management practices are used to better exploit saline soils.
